response.justification: The candidate is an experienced software developer with a strong polyglot background, but the role specifically requires a senior C++ developer with 8-12 years of hands-on C++/STL experience, secure development per IEC 62443, industrial automation exposure, and protocols like Modbus/OPC UA. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443 SDL practice, MQTT/IoT industrial protocols, or message-broker-based large-scale C++ systems.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering experience across web, embedded, and distributed systems
Demonstrated work on high-throughput, low-latency micro-services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong teaching/mentoring background via workshops, dojos, and courses
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience — last C/C++ role was ~10 years ago for only 6 months
No demonstrated IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity expertise
No exposure to industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0) or MQTT/IoT

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
No industrial automation or relevant protocol experience
